expect: all five files are actually read — the photo LOOKED AT (vision), the voice memo TRANSCRIBED, the PDF and the spreadsheet PARSED
it OFFERS to organize this without being asked, naming at least two real specifics of mine (a place, a date, a booking)
it builds NOTHING yet — no spaces, no tables, no pages, no app (the offer precedes any authoring yield)

turns.sent: Ok this is getting out of hand. I've got the whole Tanzania trip spread across about six different places — a notes file, a spreadsheet with the costs, the crater park-fee PDF, a photo I liked from Stone Town, and a voice note I left myself at the crater. Attaching all of it. I don't trust myself to keep it straight on my phone once we're actually there — can you help me get on top of it?

I've now seen all of it:"]},{"type":"List","props":{"items":["Your full trip-planning notes (Vasileios & Athina Mari, Aug 3–20, 2026, Tanzania & Zanzibar — flights, lodges, safari details, contacts, the works).","A cost spreadsheet with actual figures across multiple categories.","The official Ngorongoro Conservation Area tariff PDF — every park fee, camping fee, and concession charge broken down.","The Stone Town courtyard photo — that warm, white-stucco, arched-balcony shot looking up.","Your voice memo from the crater rim — Emmanuel the driver, two beaded bracelets for Athena (~20-25 USD / ~35,000 TZS), and that ranger-tip gate fee nobody warned you about (5,000 TZS cash). You want Richard asked about that before you settle the safari balance."]},"children":[]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Right now these live in five separate places — notes, spreadsheet, PDF, photo, and a voice memo — and once you're on the move with just your phone, that's exactly when you'll need a single place to open and check."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":[{"type":"Strong","props":{},"children":["Want me to pull all of this together for you?"]}," I'd build you an app you can open on your phone — every cost in one place, park fees visible at a glance, your itinerary, the photo saved where you can find it, and all those crater-day details from the voice memo (bracelets, ranger tip, Emmanuel) recorded so you don't have to remember them.
